title: Exclusive Time of Functions
hints: Notice that functions are properly nested like parentheses, suggesting a last-in-first-out structure to track the currently executing function.
Use a stack to keep track of active function calls and maintain a variable for the previous timestamp to calculate elapsed time.

When a function ends, pop it from the stack; the time spent is the current timestamp minus the previous timestamp, plus one. When a new function starts, subtract the elapsed time since the previous timestamp from the function currently on top of the stack.
